title: Content Digests, Near-Duplicate Evidence, Change Classes, and History · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A regional open-data publishing cooperative. They need policy-preserving Colly adoption, callback and storage ownership, offline checkpoint review, crash-safe resume, content change history, and no unexplained duplicate effects. Planned artifact: Deliver Resumable Colly Site-Quality Auditor with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate allowed domains, depth, body size, redirects, robots, rate limits, revisit, and user agent from owned policy and verify gaps.
Run equivalent fixtures through the owned engine and Colly adapter, reconcile requests and records, and document accepted differences.
Write temporary state, flush and sync under declared durability, validate, rename atomically, sync directory where required, and retain fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Colly Policy Map
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 6902 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate digest stages, exact and near identity, change classes, versions, tombstones, and deletion; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate allowed domains, depth, body size, redirects, robots, rate limits, revisit, and user agent from owned policy and verify gaps.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Colly Policy Map.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Change Types
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-change-types-ggawuf" that uses case 4012 to separate digest stages, exact and near identity, change classes, versions, tombstones, and deletion. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
